name_en: Temple Bell
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the crafts category, a Buddhist temple bell (bonshō). It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1966. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Repoussé Copper-Plate Image of the Amida Triad
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the crafts category, a repoussé copper-plate image of the Amida triad. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1968. Located in Hirayamachō, Midori Ward.

name_en: Waniguchi Gong of Sengen Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the crafts category, a waniguchi gong of Sengen Shrine. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1975. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Folk Museum.

name_en: Old Documents Related to the Fujufuse School
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the old documents category, old documents related to the Fujufuse school. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1968. Located in Hamanochō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Hara Documents
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the old documents category, the Hara documents. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2016. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Folk Museum.

name_en: Artifacts Excavated from the Nanamawarizuka Tumulus
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, artifacts excavated from the Nanamawarizuka burial mound. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1960. Located in Minami-Oyumichō, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Buried Cultural Properties Research Center.

name_en: Stone Slab Monument of Takeishi
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a stone slab monument (itabi) in Takeishi. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1960. Located in Takeishichō, Hanamigawa Ward.

name_en: Old Seto Ash-Glazed Jar with Four Lugs
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, an Old Seto ash-glazed jar with four lugs. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2001. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Folk Museum.

name_en: Tokoname Long-Necked Jar
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a Tokoname ware long-necked jar. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2001. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Folk Museum.

name_romaji: Uchino Dai-ichi Iseki Shutsudo Jinmentsuki Doban
name_en: Clay Tablet with Human Face Excavated from the Uchino No. 1 Site
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a clay tablet bearing a human face excavated from the Uchino No. 1 site. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2011. Located in Minami-Oyumichō, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Buried Cultural Properties Research Center.

name_romaji: Gojikkoku Iseki Shutsudo Tottetsuki Chūkū Enmenken tsuketari Ban Itten
name_en: Handled Hollow Round Inkstone Excavated from the Gojikkoku Site, with One Attached Dish
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a handled hollow round inkstone excavated from the Gojikkoku site, with one attached dish. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2012. Located in Minami-Oyumichō, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Buried Cultural Properties Research Center.

name_en: Clay Figurine Excavated from the Nakakanoko No. 2 Site
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a clay figurine (dogū) excavated from the Nakakanoko No. 2 site. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2013. Located in Minami-Oyumichō, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Buried Cultural Properties Research Center.

name_en: Stone Slab Monument of Konkōin Temple
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a stone slab monument (itabi) of Konkōin Temple. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2024. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Folk Museum.

name_en: Artifacts Excavated from the Kasori Shell Mound
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, artifacts excavated from the Kasori Shell Mound. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2025. Kept in Sakuragi, Wakaba Ward (within the Chiba City Kasori Shell Mound Museum) and in Minami-Oyumichō, Chūō Ward (within the Chiba City Buried Cultural Properties Research Center).

name_en: Civil Aviation Materials
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, materials related to civil aviation. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1984. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Folk Museum.

name_en: Decorative Curtain of the Ōfune Festival Float
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property, the decorative curtain of the ōfune (great boat) float. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2024. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward, within the Chiba City Folk Museum.

name_en: Kagura Dance and Kagura Manuscripts
description_en: An intangible folk cultural property comprising a kagura (sacred dance) and its kagura manuscripts. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1963. Located in Minami-Oyumichō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Inohana Castle Site (Including the Shichitennōzuka Mounds)
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the site of Inohana Castle, including the Shichitennōzuka mounds. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1960. Located in Inohana, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Chiba Shrine
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, Chiba Shrine. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1960. Located in Innai, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Precincts of Chibadera Temple
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the precincts of Chibadera Temple. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1960. Located in Chibaderachō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Gravestones of Successive Generations of the Chiba Clan
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the gravestones of successive generations of the Chiba clan. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1960. Located in Todorokichō, Inage Ward.

name_en: Arako Tumulus
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the Arako burial mound. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1961. Located in Aobachō, Chūō Ward, within Aoba-no-Mori Park.

name_en: Site of the Public Chiba Hospital
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the site of the public Chiba Hospital. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1962. Located in Chūō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Site of the Kyōritsu Hospital
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the site of the Kyōritsu Hospital. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1962. Located in Innai, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Jokan Mound
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the Jokan mound. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1968. Located in Hondachō, Midori Ward.

name_en: Gravestones of Successive Generations of the Morikawa Family
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the gravestones of successive generations of the Morikawa family. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1968. Located in Oyumichō, Chūō Ward.

name_en: Namerihashi Shell Mound
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the Namerihashi Shell Mound. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1982. Located in Ogurachō, Wakaba Ward.

name_en: Chibayama Hill
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, known as Chibayama. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1982. Located in Sonnōchō, Inage Ward.

name_en: Site of the Chiba Ochaya Goten Rest Palace
description_en: A monument designated as a historic site, the site of the Chiba Ochaya Goten rest palace. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 2004. Located in Gotenchō, Wakaba Ward.

name_en: Pine Grove of Inage
description_en: A monument designated as a place of scenic beauty, the pine grove of Inage. It was municipally designated by Chiba City, with designation dating to 1961. Located in Inage, Inage Ward.

name_en: Image of the Horse-Headed Kannon Bosatsu
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the buildings category, an image of the horse-headed Kannon (Batō Kanzeon Bosatsu). It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 1992-12-08. Located at Nekoya aza Hiro 337-2.

name_romaji: Tenshō Kenchichō: Shimōsa no Kuni Shirai-no-shō Mochikusa-mura On-nawaire Mizuchō
name_en: Tenshō-Era Land Survey Register of Mochikusa Village, Shirai Manor, Shimōsa Province
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the old documents category, a Tenshō-era land survey register of Mochikusa village, Shirai manor, Shimōsa Province. It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 1990-02-23. Kept at the Yachimata City Folk Museum.

name_romaji: Hōei Gannen Seita-mura Yōsō-mura Jizakai Sōron Saikyosho
name_en: Adjudication Document of the Boundary Dispute between Seita and Yōsō Villages in the First Year of Hōei
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, an adjudication document concerning the land boundary dispute between Seita and Yōsō villages in the first year of the Hōei era. It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 1991-03-28. Kept at the Yachimata City Folk Museum.

name_en: Painting of Horse Catching
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, a painting depicting horse catching. It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 1995-03-24. Privately held.

name_en: Preparatory Sketch for the Painting of Horse Catching
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, a preparatory sketch for the painting of horse catching. It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 1995-03-24. Privately held.

name_en: Ridge-End Ogre Tile of Nagasawa Shrine
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, a ridge-end ogre tile (onigawara) of Nagasawa Shrine. It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 1995-03-24. Kept at Nishū Elementary School.

name_en: Battle Surcoat Used by Maeyama Seiichirō
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the historical materials category, a battle surcoat (jinbaori) used by Maeyama Seiichirō. It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 2021-07-20. Kept at the Yachimata City Folk Museum.

name_romaji: Koeppa no Benten no Shimōsa-gata Itabi
name_en: Shimōsa-Type Stone Slab Monument of the Koeppa Benten
description_en: A tangible cultural property in the archaeological materials category, a Shimōsa-type stone slab monument (itabi) at the Koeppa Benten. It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 2023-10-04. Located at Hijikai aza Dainoshita 81-1.

name_en: Framed Votive Picture 'Painting of Horse Catching'
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property, a framed votive picture (ema) titled 'Toba no Zu' (painting of horse catching). It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 1992-12-08. Located at the Okada Batō Kanzeon Hall.

name_en: Banners of the Okada Horse-Headed Kannon
description_en: A tangible folk cultural property, banners (nobori) of the Okada horse-headed Kannon. It was municipally designated by Yachimata City on 1998-03-24. Held by Hōsenji Temple.
